description: This neuron detects single capital letters or short capitalized abbreviations at the beginning of proper nouns, brand names, technical terms, or specialized vocabulary.
This neuron detects capitalized single letters or short letter sequences at the beginning of proper nouns, names, titles, or technical terms.
This neuron detects the first token of proper nouns, brand names, technical terms, and titles, particularly at the beginning of sentences or entries.
This neuron detects proper nouns and capitalized words that begin sentences or titles, particularly names of people, places, organizations, and technical terms.
This neuron appears to detect capital letters that begin proper nouns, brand names, or significant words at the start of titles, names, or new sections.
This neuron detects the first letter of proper nouns, brand names, or technical terms at the beginning of sentences or text segments.
This neuron detects the first letter or initial character of a proper noun, brand name, or title at the beginning of a text segment or sentence.
This neuron detects the beginning of a new line or block of text, particularly after a newline character or at the start of content following formatting/whitespace.
This neuron detects the beginning of a new line or text block, particularly at the start of titles, headings, code blocks, or article/section beginnings.
This neuron detects opening comment delimiters and formatting markers, particularly asterisks or angle brackets that begin code comments, HTML tags, or special formatting sections.
This neuron detects the beginning of a new line or section, particularly at the start of titles, headings, code blocks, or after line breaks in formatted text.
This neuron detects the beginning of a new line or empty space at the start of formatting/code blocks, particularly in technical contexts like XML, code snippets, or forum responses.
This neuron detects the word "divisor" when it appears in mathematical contexts asking for the greatest/highest common divisor (GCD) of two numbers.
This neuron detects decimal points in numeric literals and also newline characters in code or structured data contexts.
This neuron detects whitespace (particularly spaces or newlines) that precedes colons, closing tags, or other punctuation marks in code or markup contexts.
The neuron detects hyphens or dashes used to connect related terms, particularly in technical specifications, file paths, or compound descriptors.
Line breaks or paragraph breaks in formatted text, particularly occurring before new sections, headings, or topic transitions.
The neuron detects the beginning of XML/HTML closing tags or angle brackets that precede tag names or whitespace in markup language syntax.
This neuron detects line breaks or newlines, particularly in contexts where text is transitioning between quoted content, user comments, or separate discussion threads in online forums.

This neuron detects newline characters that immediately follow specific punctuation marks or keywords (particularly colons, HTML/XML opening tags, and quote marks), typically indicating the start of a new block of content or code.
A period marking the end of a sentence that introduces or concludes an academic, technical, or formal topic, often preceding explanatory details or context.
This neuron detects period tokens that mark the end of formal titles, headings, or descriptive phrases in academic, technical, or professional documents.
Periods that appear in version numbers, package/namespace paths, URLs, and sentence-ending punctuation.
This neuron detects the start of a new line or section following a heading, title, or introductory label, often marking the beginning of content after metadata or structural markers.
The neuron detects line breaks that follow formal headers or signature-like elements, particularly those ending a document section or organizational identifier.
This neuron detects sentence-ending periods, particularly those that conclude declarative statements or mark the end of a complete thought before transitioning to new information.
This neuron detects periods that end sentences, particularly when they conclude informational or descriptive statements and are followed by the start of a new sentence or section.

This neuron detects periods that mark the end of formal academic or technical titles, article headlines, or formal descriptive phrases, particularly in written contexts where a title or heading is being concluded before transitional or explanatory content begins.
This neuron detects sentence-ending periods that appear after technical or academic article titles, particularly in contexts where the title is followed by an abstract or body text.
This neuron detects Japanese hiragana characters and grammatical particles, particularly in the context of Japanese text snippets and Ruby annotations (furigana).
